sonnet_review.biasedText: The three-way battle between Democrats running for Senate in Illinois has evolved into an argument about the best way to combat President Trump's deportation agenda.
sonnet_review.reason: The author’s framing (“combat” and “deportation agenda”) can tilt adversarially toward Trump, though it may also reflect the candidates’ stated posture.
“Agenda” and the focus on “deportation” carry negative connotations; a more neutral phrasing exists, though this wording is common in political reporting.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Democratic anger over Mr. Trump's deportation policies led to a shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security when its funding expired this weekend.
sonnet_review.reason: Attributing causation to “Democratic anger” simplifies a complex funding dispute and emphasizes one side’s emotion, which can reflect slanted framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: The three Senate candidates, who aren’t split by major ideological or policy differences, now offer a window into how Democrats could approach dismantling Mr. Trump’s immigration enforcement structure if they win back full control in Washington.
gpt_review.reason: This is a descriptive summary of the race; it does not clearly minimize differences beyond reporting the author’s assessment.

gpt_review.biasedText: Democratic anger over Mr. Trump’s deportation policies led to a shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security when its funding expired this weekend.
gpt_review.reason: Singling out “Democratic anger” as the cause highlights partisan blame and negative consequences, which can be a biased framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: And yet ambitious Democrats who saw how calls to “abolish ICE” and “defund the police” hurt the party in recent years are concerned that running on a definitive message to abolish the agency is risky politics — if not in 2026 then beyond.
gpt_review.reason: The claim that these slogans “hurt the party” is asserted without evidence and presented as a given, reflecting evaluative language in the author’s voice.
